title: Relativity Space to privately develop Mars orbiter mission
summary: Relativity Space announced plans to launch a Mars orbiter mission in 2028, marking its entry into privately developed planetary missions. This initiative expands the company's focus beyond Earth-based launches.

title: Upgraded Ariane 6 launches Amazon Leo satellites
summary: An upgraded Ariane 6 rocket successfully launched 36 Amazon Leo satellites on June 17. The European Space Agency (ESA) is considering options to increase the rocket's launch frequency.

title: America’s next economic frontier is 240,000 miles away
summary: The article discusses the economic potential of the Moon as a frontier for America, highlighting its significance 240,000 miles away. It references the historical Apollo missions and the renewed interest in lunar exploration.

title: China conducts 4 launches in 3 days, but silence follows Kuaizhou–11 launch
summary: China conducted four launches in three days, but the Kuaizhou-11 solid rocket launch was followed by a long silence, indicating potential issues.

title: Dawn Aerospace raises $25 million
summary: Dawn Aerospace has secured $25 million in funding to expand its in-space transportation and suborbital spaceplane operations. The investment will support scaling up the company's innovative space technologies.

title: Look Up and Skynopy partner on automated satellite collision avoidance service
summary: Look Up and Skynopy have partnered to develop an automated satellite collision avoidance service using Skynopy's ground station network. The collaboration aims to enhance safety in low Earth orbit by leveraging automated systems.

title: June 25:  Golden Dome: How Could Sensors Protect the United States?
summary: The article discusses the Golden Dome satellite program and the sensor technologies used to protect the United States. It explores the operational capabilities of these satellites and potential future developments.

title: Astrobotic unveils Griffin-1 lunar lander
summary: Astrobotic has revealed its Griffin-1 lunar lander, which is set to launch later this year as part of NASA's lunar base initiatives. The lander will serve as a key component in advancing the agency's ambitions for a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Swiss decision to not contribute to Copernicus tests program’s value model
summary: Switzerland has decided not to participate in the EU's Copernicus Earth observation program for the 2028–2034 funding cycle, sparking discussions about the program's value. The move highlights broader debates over international contributions to European space initiatives.

title: The SpaceNews space unicorn tracker
summary: SpaceNews has published a tracker listing private space companies valued at $1 billion or more, known as 'space unicorns'. This initiative highlights the growing prominence of high-value private space ventures.

title: One of these twin stars has likely been snacking on exoplanets
summary: Astronomers found chemical differences between twin stars suggesting one has consumed at least one exoplanet. This discovery sheds light on planetary dynamics in binary star systems.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers extreme exoplanet being roasted by its home star
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope discovered an extreme exoplanet being intensely heated by its host star, described as a 'Hot Jupiter'. This finding highlights the extreme conditions of such planets orbiting close to their stars.

title: 'Warframe''s new Constellations update puts the focus back among the stars (interview)
summary: Digital Extremes' latest 'Warframe' update, Constellations, revitalizes the game with a space-themed focus, blending old mechanics with new ideas.

title: A volcanic eruption seen from space | Space photo of the day for June 17, 2026
summary: A volcanic eruption on Hawaii's Kīlauea volcano was captured from space, marking its 49th known lava outbreak. The event was highlighted in a space photo of the day for June 17, 2026.

title: 'Best. Mars. Mission. Ever.' Scientists hail MAVEN's legacy as NASA retires Red Planet orbiter
summary: NASA's MAVEN Mars mission concluded after 11 years, uncovering how Mars lost its atmosphere and aiding surface mission communications. Scientists praised it as a groundbreaking achievement.

title: Stream Season 3 of Silo in safety, anywhere, with 70% off 24 months of ProtonVPN Plus
summary: ProtonVPN is offering a 70% discount on 24 months of ProtonVPN Plus, allowing users to stream Season 3 of Silo safely. The deal promotes online security and access to the upcoming show.

title: NASA discussing bold mission to boost Swift space telescope today: Listen live
summary: NASA is planning an ambitious mission to boost the orbit of its Swift space telescope. The details will be discussed in a press conference today.

title: NASA X-ray spacecraft catches jet erupting from 1st supermassive black hole imaged by humanity
summary: NASA's X-ray spacecraft has captured a detailed image of a jet erupting from the first supermassive black hole ever imaged by humanity. This observation reveals unprecedented changes in the jet's structure.

title: NASA Announces Public-Private Partnership to Advance Mars Science
summary: NASA announced a public-private partnership with Relativity Space to advance Mars science by combining NASA's instruments with Relativity's spacecraft and launch capabilities. The collaboration aims to deliver the Aeolus atmospheric-science payload suite to Mars.

title: Search for Hidden Cosmic Companions in Sun’s Backyard
summary: Astronomers are searching for hidden cosmic companions, such as brown dwarfs, which orbit stars and help reveal their ages. These brown dwarfs are too massive to be planets but too low-mass to be stars.

title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211, resembling a swarm of bees. The cluster serves as a gravitational lens, revealing distant galaxies and aiding in the study of the universe's evolution.

title: NASA’s Webb Catches Exoplanet Getting Roasted
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope observed an exoplanet, HD 80606 b, experiencing extreme heating due to its elliptical orbit. Researchers analyzed the data to study the planet's atmospheric response to intense stellar radiation.

title: Astronaut Jessica Meir Assists With Hardware Updates for NASA’s Cold Atom Lab
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir installed hardware updates to the Cold Atom Lab (CAL) aboard the International Space Station. CAL chills atoms to near absolute zero for quantum experiments.

title: NASA Webb, Hubble Reveal History of Relic of Milky Way’s Formation
summary: Researchers used NASA's James Webb and Hubble Space Telescopes to reveal that Terzan 5 is not a globular star cluster but offers insights into galaxy formation. This discovery challenges previous classifications and enhances understanding of the Milky Way's evolution.

title: NASA’s Quantum Lab Aboard Space Station Gets Chilly Upgrade
summary: NASA's Cold Atom Lab on the International Space Station has been upgraded to enhance quantum experiments in microgravity. The facility enables breakthroughs in fundamental physics and quantum technology research.

title: NASA Uses Machine Learning to Enhance Flash Flood Warnings
summary: NASA has developed a machine learning system called TACLS to improve flash flood warnings by analyzing data from satellite networks. The system assists meteorologists at the National Weather Service in making more efficient flood forecasts.

title: Department of Health and Human Services Digital Stockpile & Manufacturing Response Network Challenge
summary: NASA's Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI) is assisting the Department of Health and Human Services in a crowdsourcing challenge for stockpile and manufacturing response. The NASA Tournament Lab will run the external challenge under CoECI's contract capability.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space's Terran R second stage has been transported to Stennis Space Center as the company progresses toward its planned 2026 debut of the heavy-lift vehicle. This marks a key step in the development of the fully reusable rocket.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, indicating a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks ago.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. This marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This marks a significant milestone in the structural verification of the rocket.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. This crewed mission marks a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing for Booster 20, preparing for the upcoming Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week includes space launches from China, Rocket Lab, and multiple Starlink missions. Flights are scheduled from locations in China, California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news source is a B2B website focused on the technology, engineering, and construction industries. It may cover various sectors, including space industry developments.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions by the European Space Agency.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm developed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential to revolutionize in-space operations. The technology is positioned as a key advancement for satellite servicing and orbital debris management.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks the resumption of routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. The programme facilitates participation of research institutes in European space science missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are assisting ESA in testing and selecting materials for spacecraft to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their innovative approaches are helping to space-proof the agency's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test is essential for ensuring the parachute's functionality during re-entry.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly participate in lunar surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans its first GEO mission in 2027, following a near-Earth mission launching this month.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace, a New Zealand-Dutch space transportation company, has raised $25 million in a Series B funding round. The funds will be used to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera raised £3 million to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance sovereign technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with AFRL to develop AI anomaly detection technology, currently testing it on terrestrial flat sats with plans to expand into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a decline in stocks of other publicly traded space companies, causing a broad pullback among investors. The dip affected major players across the space industry.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that a SpaceX IPO could significantly boost the broader space industry by increasing investor confidence and capital flow. The move could shift the industry's reliance from private funding to public markets.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about the kind of economy that will emerge. The largest IPO in history is poised to drive record investment.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. CEO Eric Sung described it as a defining moment for Intellian.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a recently formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to enhance its satellite offerings. The acquisition follows Lyntris's formation through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund, led by Jeff Bezos, is investing $26 million to support the development of the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite system. The investment is part of a broader coalition backing the non-profit initiative.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: The article features an interview with Tina Ghataore, a former Satellite Executive of the Year, discussing future developments in the space industry. It also includes breaking news related to the interview.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for a robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying it to prioritize developing space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following recent U.S. Space developments.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security's Science and Technology Directorate has unveiled new space cybersecurity research to safeguard critical infrastructure. This effort is part of the Aerospace SPARTA framework aimed at enhancing space security.

title: The Space Sector Under Cyber Fire in the US-Israel-Iran War
summary: The U.S. and Israel launched Operation Epic Fury, involving airstrikes and cyber operations against Iran, amid escalating tensions. The space sector is highlighted as a potential target in this conflict.

title: SpaceX Launches 3 AST SpaceMobile BlueBirds in Stacked Mission
summary: SpaceX successfully launched three AST SpaceMobile BlueBird satellites in a stacked configuration during a single mission. This marks the first time three BlueBird satellites were deployed together in a launch fairing.

title: Eutelsat Secures First Order Under French Defense Agreement
summary: Eutelsat has secured its first order under a new defense agreement with the French Ministry of the Armed Forces and Veterans. The company also announced additional deals, including one for Low-Earth Orbit services.

title: All.Space Relocates to US Amid York Space Acquisition Process
summary: All.Space has moved its operations from the U.K. to Alabama, USA, aligning with U.S. defense priorities as it undergoes acquisition by York Space Systems. The satellite terminal developer emphasizes its commitment to the U.S. market during this transition.
